Navigation

On This Page

Workflows

Create React App Existing Project Runtime Dependency Dev Dependency React Packages Fix Installation Fix ESLint Plugin Inspect and Update Remove Package Run Scripts

Reference

npm Basics Dependency Types Common Packages ESLint Fixes package.json Scripts node_modules Package Information Environment Variables Python Packages Common Errors Typical Workflow

Project Setup

Packages & Dependencies

The fastest reference for getting a JavaScript project running, installing the right packages, fixing dependency problems, and checking the scripts you need before you commit.

Official npm Docs →

Highest Priority

Daily Dependency Workflows

These cards are organized around the tasks developers actually do: start a project, install packages, repair broken dependencies, and run the scripts that confirm the app still works.

New App

Create a New React App

Vite 
npm create vite@latest client

Select a framework:
React

Select a variant:
JavaScript

cd client
npm install
npm run dev

This is the full flow from project creation to a running development server, including the interactive choices Vite asks for in the terminal.

Existing Codebase

Start an Existing Project

Clone 
git clone <repository-url>
cd <project-folder>
npm install
npm run dev

npm install reads package.json, installs the required dependencies into node_modules, and prepares the project for the local dev server.

Runtime Package

Install a Runtime Dependency

App 
npm install axios

import axios from "axios";

Regular dependencies are packages your application needs while it runs in the browser or on the server.

Tooling

Install a Development Dependency

-D 
npm install -D eslint
npm install -D prettier
npm install -D cypress

-D is short for --save-dev. Use it for development, testing, formatting, and linting tools rather than packages the app needs in production.

React Setup

Add Common React Packages

Frontend 
npm install axios
npm install react-router-dom
npm install clsx
npm install uuid

axios is commonly used for HTTP requests.

react-router-dom handles client-side routes and navigation.

clsx helps build conditional class names.

uuid generates unique IDs for client data.

Repair

Fix a Broken Dependency Installation

Cleanup 
rm -rf node_modules
npm install
rm -rf node_modules
rm package-lock.json
npm install
Delete package-lock.json only as the more aggressive cleanup option. It should not be the first step because it can install different package versions.

Linting Fix

Fix a Missing ESLint Plugin

Cypress 
npm install -D eslint-plugin-cypress
import cypress from "eslint-plugin-cypress";

export default [
  {
    files: ["cypress/**/*.{js,jsx}"],
    extends: [cypress.configs.recommended],
  },
];

Use the install command first, then update eslint.config.js so ESLint knows how to lint Cypress test files.

Inspect

Inspect and Update Packages

Check 
npm list --depth=0
npm outdated
npm view axios version
npm update

Check what is installed, see what is outdated, compare against the newest published version, then update within the version ranges your project already allows.

Cleanup

Remove a Package

Uninstall 
npm uninstall package-name

This removes the package from both node_modules and package.json, so the dependency is gone from your install and your manifest.

Verification

Run Project Scripts

Scripts 
npm run dev
npm run lint
npm test
npm run build

These commands come from the scripts section of package.json. Use them after package changes to prove the app still starts, lints, tests, and builds.

Package Manager

npm Basics

Install Project Dependencies

npm install

Reads package.json and installs the project's dependencies into node_modules.

Install One Package

npm install axios

Installs the package and adds it to dependencies.

Install a Development Tool

npm install --save-dev eslint

Installs the package and adds it to devDependencies.

Short Development Flag

npm install -D prettier

-D is the shorter version of --save-dev.

Remove a Package

npm uninstall axios

Removes the package from node_modules and package.json.

Install Globally

npm install -g package-name

Installs a command-line package globally instead of inside one project.

Most project packages should be installed locally. Use global installation only when the tool is intended to be available as a system-wide terminal command.

package.json

Dependency Types

Application Code

dependencies

Packages needed while the application is running.

axios react react-router-dom express 
npm install axios

Development Tools

devDependencies

Packages used while developing, testing, formatting, or building the project.

eslint prettier vitest cypress 
npm install -D eslint

Example package.json

{
  "dependencies": {
    "axios": "^1.0.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-router-dom": "^7.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "eslint": "^9.0.0",
    "prettier": "^3.0.0",
    "vitest": "^3.0.0"
  }
}

Frontend Development

Common Packages

HTTP Requests

Axios

npm install axios

Send GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE requests.

Navigation

React Router

npm install react-router-dom

Add client-side routes and navigation to a React application.

Class Names

clsx

npm install clsx

Apply CSS class names conditionally.

Unique Values

UUID

npm install uuid

Generate unique identifiers for objects or records.

Testing

Vitest

npm install -D vitest

Run unit tests in modern JavaScript and Vite projects.

Browser Testing

Cypress

npm install -D cypress

Test user interactions inside a real browser.

Code Quality

ESLint

npm install -D eslint

Find JavaScript and React code problems.

Formatting

Prettier

npm install -D prettier

Format JavaScript, JSON, CSS, Markdown, and other files.

Linting Fixes

ESLint Plugins

!

ESLint Cannot Find the Cypress Plugin

Install the missing plugin, import it into the ESLint configuration, and apply it to Cypress files.

1. Install the Plugin

npm install -D eslint-plugin-cypress

2. Import the Plugin

import cypress from "eslint-plugin-cypress";

3. Add the Cypress Configuration

export default [
  {
    files: ["cypress/**/*.{js,jsx}"],
    extends: [cypress.configs.recommended],
  },
];
The exact configuration can vary depending on the ESLint configuration format and plugin version used by the project. Keep the version that works with your course or repository.

Install ESLint

npm install -D eslint

Run ESLint

npx eslint .

Automatically Fix Issues

npx eslint . --fix

Run the Project Script

npm run lint

Common ESLint Plugins

eslint-plugin-cypress Cypress testing rules
eslint-plugin-react React component rules
eslint-plugin-react-hooks React Hooks rules
eslint-plugin-import Import and export rules

Automation

package.json Scripts

Scripts create short commands for starting, testing, formatting, linting, and building a project.

Example Scripts

{
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest",
    "lint": "eslint .",
    "format": "prettier . --write"
  }
}

Run the Scripts

npm run dev Start Vite
npm run build Create the production build
npm test Run tests
npm run lint Check ESLint rules
npm run format Format project files

Dependency Storage

node_modules

📁

node_modules

Contains the packages installed for the current project.

JSON

package.json

Lists project information, scripts, and dependency ranges.

LOCK

package-lock.json

Records the exact dependency versions installed.

Do not commit node_modules to Git. Add it to .gitignore.

Delete and Reinstall

rm -rf node_modules
npm install

Full Clean Reinstall

rm -rf node_modules
rm package-lock.json
npm install
Deleting package-lock.json can install different package versions. Try deleting only node_modules first.

Exact Lockfile Install

npm ci

npm ci removes the existing node_modules folder and installs the exact versions in package-lock.json.

Inspect and Update

Package Information

Installed Packages

npm list Show the dependency tree
npm list --depth=0 Show only top-level packages

Outdated Packages

npm outdated Compare current and available versions
npm update Update packages within allowed ranges

Package Details

npm view axios version See the latest version
npm view axios See package information

Find Why a Package Exists

npm explain package-name Explain which dependency installed it
npm list package-name Find it in the dependency tree

Configuration

Environment Variables

.env

VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3000
VITE_APP_NAME=My Application

Read in Vite

const apiUrl = import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL;

console.log(apiUrl);
Variables exposed to frontend code are visible to users. Never place passwords, private keys, or secret API credentials in a frontend environment variable.

Ignore Local Environment Files

# .gitignore
.env
.env.local

Python Tools

Python Packages

Install a Package

pip install package-name

Install a Python package into the active environment.

Install Requirements

pip install -r requirements.txt

Install all packages listed in the requirements file.

Save Installed Packages

pip freeze > requirements.txt

Write the current environment's installed packages to a file.

See Installed Packages

pip list

Display packages installed in the active environment.

Common Python Development Tools

Formatting

Black

pip install black

Linting

Pylint

pip install pylint

Testing

pytest

pip install pytest

Command-Line Tool

pipx

pipx install black

Troubleshooting

Common Errors and Fixes

!

Module Not Found

The project dependencies may not be installed.

npm install
!

Missing ESLint Plugin

Install the plugin named in the error message.

npm install -D eslint-plugin-cypress
!

Broken node_modules

Delete installed packages and reinstall them.

rm -rf node_modules
npm install
!

Command Not Found

Run a local package command through npx.

npx eslint .
npx prettier . --write
!

Port Already in Use

Find the process using the port and stop it.

lsof -i :5173
kill -9 PID
!

npm Script Missing

Check the scripts available in package.json.

npm run
Read the first useful error message before reinstalling everything. The error often names the exact missing package, file, script, or configuration.

New Project Setup

Typical Package Workflow

1

Open the Project

Make sure the terminal is inside the folder containing package.json.

↓
2

Install Existing Dependencies

npm install
↓
3

Install Additional Packages

npm install axios npm install -D eslint-plugin-cypress
↓
4

Update Configuration

Add the required ESLint plugin, script, import, or environment variable.

↓
5

Run the Project

npm run dev
↓
6

Run Quality Checks

npm run lint npm test npm run build
↓
7

Commit Package Files

Commit package.json and package-lock.json, but not node_modules.

Return to all cheat sheets
